mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of periods when RSAFS 3-month annualized growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a critical threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also elevated, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their respective thresholds, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion indicate robust investment and future demand, which is a positive signal for overall economic health and thus S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods during economic expansions when HOUST is above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power is not significantly different from zero in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a critical level indicates a contraction in manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling broader economic weakness and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer confidence, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of periods when PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment supporting robust economic expansion, leading to strong S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of periods where GDPC1 is strong and FEDFUNDS is low, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold creates uncertainty about future monetary policy and erodes purchasing power, leading to negative sentiment and lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the average return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
